pattern: The longest path through any node is the sum of its two child depths, so one height-computing pass can track the best such sum seen anywhere.
hints: The longest path does not have to touch the root; it can sit entirely inside a subtree.
At any node, the longest path bending through it equals the height of its left side plus the height of its right side.
Compute heights recursively, and while doing so keep a global maximum of leftHeight + rightHeight measured in edges.

edge_inputs.description: longest path bypasses the root entirely
two deep branches of equal length
